title: Fisherman's Song Ahoy!
description: A Grenadian Calypso song about a fisherman going out to sea and the joy of bringing home a catch for his family.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Sunrise peekin', boat a-waitin'
Net is ready, no debatin'
[CLAP] Jump inside, feel the spray
Today a good catch, we go pray!

[Chorus]
Row, row, row the boat so steady
[SNAP] Fish are jumpin', feeling ready
Sunshine warm, the sea so blue
[STOMP] Fisherman's song, for me and you!

[Verse 2]
Seagulls flyin', overhead
Lookin' for scraps, neatly spread
Cast the net, wide and far
Hopin' to catch a silver star!

[Chorus]
Row, row, row the boat so steady
[SNAP] Fish are jumpin', feeling ready
Sunshine warm, the sea so blue
[STOMP] Fisherman's song, for me and you!

[Verse 3]
Pullin' it in, feel the weight
Filled with fish, it's truly great!
Snapper, grouper, all so bright
Tonight, we feast with all our might!

[Chorus]
Row, row, row the boat so steady
[SNAP] Fish are jumpin', feeling ready
Sunshine warm, the sea so blue
[STOMP] Fisherman's song, for me and you!

[Verse 4]
Back to shore, the sun is low
Family waitin', watchin' us go.
"Aye!" I shout, "We bringin' home the eats!"
[CLAP] A big Grenadian family feast!
thumbnailPrompt: Vibrant and colorful cartoon illustration depicting a Grenadian fisherman in a brightly painted wooden boat on turquoise Caribbean waters. He is smiling and pulling a net full of colorful cartoon fish (snapper, grouper). In the background, a Grenadian village with brightly colored houses nestled amongst lush green hills, palm trees swaying in the breeze, and children dancing on the beach with miniature steelpans. Include musical notes floating in the air. The sky is a bright blue with fluffy white clouds. 'Fisherman's Song Ahoy!' title is prominently displayed.
educationalValue: Children learn about the importance of fishing in Grenadian culture, family meals, different types of fish, and Grenadian expressions.
